rationale: The cited paper directly studies an affective-state × message-framing interaction on persuasion, and the citing paper’s core question is also about how preexisting emotion interacts with ad framing to change persuasion outcomes. In the citing note, the authors explicitly position their contribution as extending prior emotion-and-persuasion compatibility work and identifying boundary conditions, making this cited work a plausible theoretical/comparison source rather than a generic mention.
